data.title: Accenture to acquire Cabel Industry to strengthen core banking services in Italy
Europe markets majorly closed for Christmas holidays
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.01% at 9,888. 

Germany (DAX:IND) closed.

France (CAC:IND) closed.

In other parts of Europe, the Netherlands’ economy expanded by 0.5% Q/Q in Q3.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.05% higher to 589 on Wednesday as investors head into the holiday mood. Major indexes operated on shortened trading hours, and few bourses across the globe stayed closed on Christmas Eve. 

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was almost flat at 4.17%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.52%. 

Germany's bond market closed.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Intellistake Technologies to buy Singularity Venture Hub
data.description:
• Intellistake Technologies (ISTKF) to acquire Singularity Venture Hub ((SVH)) in a deal that values the acquisition at ~C$25 million.
  • Singularity Venture Hub manages over $90 million USD in digital assets and has incubated projects valued at $250 million USD, focusing on AI-Web3 incubation, treasury management, and advisory services.
  • The deal involves the issuance of about ~14M Intellistake (ISTKF) common shares priced at C$1.78 each to acquire 100% of SVH's outstanding securities. 
  • Upon closing, Mario Casiraghi would be appointed to the Intellistake Board of Directors and also be appointed as President of the company.

data.title: PAG and KKR to acquire Sapporo Real Estate in phased three-year deal for ¥477B
data.description:
PAG, a leading Asia-based alternative investment firm and KKR (KKR) have signed definitive agreements to acquire 100% of Sapporo Real Estate Co., Ltd. from Sapporo Holdings (SOOBF).

Sapporo Holdings said that the consideration amount for the sale of Sapporo Real Estate Co., Ltd. to the consortium led by PAG and KKR is 477 billion yen (approximately $3.1 billion USD).

The transaction features a phased handover over three years to maintain operational stability across key locations like Ebisu (Tokyo) and Sapporo (Hokkaido). The deal begins with an initial 51% stake acquisition expected by June 2026. The remaining shares will be acquired in stages, eventually resulting in 100% ownership by the PAG and KKR funds.

Sapporo Holdings has a history of over 140 years and manages businesses in alcoholic beverages, food and soft drinks, and real estate. The company plans to focus on its alcoholic beverages sector, where it has strengths, to grow as a capital-efficient company that provides great beverage experiences globally.

To do this, Sapporo Holdings will sell its real estate business to funds managed by PAG and KKR, allowing it to concentrate on its alcoholic beverages initiatives.

Sapporo Real Estate, which manages various properties in Ebisu and Sapporo, will become independent after the sale.

PAG and KKR will use their expertise and networks to enhance Sapporo Real Estate’s portfolio and support community-focused urban development.

KKR is investing mainly through its Asia real estate strategy, pending regulatory approvals.

"We are pleased to collaborate with PAG to support the Company’s next stage of growth, and look forward to sharing our global network, investment experience and deep operational expertise in development, operations, and hospitality across KKR’s global platform,” said Hiro Hirano, Deputy Executive Chairman of KKR Asia Pacific and CEO of KKR Japan.

data.title: BP to sell 65% stake in Castrol to Stonepeak
data.description:
BP (BP) to sell a majority controlling interest, 65% in Castrol to alternative investment firm Stonepeak, valuing the lubricant unit at $10.1 billion.

BP (BP) will retain a 35% minority stake, while the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board contributes up to $1.05 billion for an indirect stake. Previous reports indicate that BP (BP) may sell the stake after a two-year lock-in period.

Talks between the two companies emerged in November 2025 as part of BP's (BP) $20 billion divestment push, with early valuations around $8 billion.

BP (BP) would get about $6 billion for a 65% stake in the lubricants business, the WSJ earlier reported.

data.title: Sanofi to buy Dynavax for $15.50 per share
data.description:
• Sanofi (SNY) to acquire Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) for $15.50 per share in cash, valuing the company at approximately $2.2 billion—a 39% premium to its December 23 closing price. 
  • This deal adds Dynavax's marketed HEPLISAV-B adult hepatitis B vaccine and phase 1/2 shingles candidate Z-1018 to Sanofi's portfolio, enhancing its adult immunization offerings.
  • Sanofi (SNY) plans to fund the acquisition with available cash resources.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 of 2026.
  • If the tender offer is successfully completed, a wholly owned subsidiary of Sanofi (SNY) will merge with and into Dynavax (DVAX), and all the outstanding Dynavax shares that are not tendered will be converted into the right to receive the same $15.50 per share in cash offered to Dynavax (DVAX) shareholders.

data.title: CB&I to acquire Petrofac’s Asset Solutions business
Lemonade is the best performing property and casualty insurance stock YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about +14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of the top 10 property and casualty insurance stocks ranked by their YTD performance in descending order. Additional data point included is their SA Quant ratings.

The list is topped by Lemonade (LMND), with a YTD performance of +124.97%. HCI Group (HCI) and Hamilton Insurance Group (HG) are next, with YTD performances of +66.39% and +49.08% respectively. Mercury General (MCY) and Palomar Holdings (PLMR) round out the top five.

Several top-performing stocks on this list have earned high Quant ratings, specifically “Strong Buy” designations. These include HCI Group (HCI) with a rating of 4.84, Hamilton Insurance Group (HG) at 4.74, The Hartford Insurance Group (HIG) at 4.69, and Mercury General (MCY) at 4.62.

Here is the list:

  1. Lemonade (LMND), YTD perf: +124.97%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.45)

  2. HCI Group (HCI), YTD perf: +66.39%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.84)

  3. Hamilton Insurance Group (HG), YTD perf: +49.08%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.74)

  4. Mercury General (MCY), YTD perf: +43.32%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.62)

  5. Palomar Holdings (PLMR), YTD perf: +29.43%, Quant Rating: Buy (4.35)

  6. Old Republic International (ORI), YTD perf: +27.63%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.85)

  7. Loews (L), YTD perf: +27.62%

  8. The Hartford Insurance Group (HIG), YTD perf: +27.56%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.69)

  9. Markel Group (MKL), YTD perf: +26.44%, Quant Rating: Buy (4.23)

  10. AXIS Capital Holdings (AXS), YTD perf: +22.95%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.35)

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: Prudential is the best YTD performer among life and health insurance stocks
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about +14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of the top 10 life and health insurance stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes SA Quant ratings for each stock.

The list is topped by Prudential (PUK), with a YTD performance of +96.80%. Lincoln National (LNC) and Brighthouse Financial (BHF) are next, rounding out the top three performers in the sector.

Among the notable stocks on the list, Aegon (AEG), ranking 5th, holds a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.69, making it the highest-rated stock on the list.
